description: Phase 11: Data Plane Sync, Offline-First Patterns, and Reliability. Stabilize the sync-engine with optimistic update support, enhance conflict resolution with a user-facing conflict UI data model, add retry/backoff standards, add data retention and archival utilities, and create an offline-first operation queue that integrates with the service worker manager.
steps: Step 1: Create packages/sync-engine/src/optimistic-updates.ts - Implement OptimisticUpdateManager class that maintains a pending mutations list. Methods: apply(mutationId, optimisticState) to instantly apply UI state, confirm(mutationId) to finalize, rollback(mutationId) to revert. Track pending count and provide subscribe() for UI status binding. Export from packages/sync-engine/src/index.ts.

Step 2: Create packages/sync-engine/src/offline-queue.ts - Implement OfflineOperationQueue that extends the existing LocalStore queue. Add priority ordering, deduplication by operation key, max age expiration, and batch replay with configurable concurrency. Integrate with ServiceWorkerManager for background sync trigger. Export from packages/sync-engine/src/index.ts.

Step 3: Create packages/sync-engine/src/retry-backoff.ts - Implement RetryWithBackoff utility class. Configurable: maxRetries, initialDelayMs, maxDelayMs, backoffMultiplier, jitter (boolean). Methods: execute(fn) wraps an async function with retry logic. shouldRetry(error) determines if error is retryable. getDelay(attempt) calculates delay with exponential backoff and optional jitter. Export from packages/sync-engine/src/index.ts.

Step 4: Enhance packages/sync-engine/src/conflict-resolution.ts - Add ConflictRecord interface (id, documentId, localValue, remoteValue, localTimestamp, remoteTimestamp, strategy, resolvedValue, resolvedAt, resolvedBy). Add ConflictStore class that records conflicts for UI display. Methods: record(conflict), resolve(id, resolution), getPending(), getHistory(documentId). Export from packages/sync-engine/src/index.ts.

Step 5: Create packages/data-plane/src/data-retention.ts - Implement DataRetentionPolicy class with configurable rules per entity type (retention period, archive strategy, hard-delete vs soft-delete). Methods: evaluate(records) returns which records to archive/delete. createArchiveBatch(records) prepares records for cold storage. Add RetentionScheduler that can be run periodically. Export from packages/data-plane/src/index.ts.

Step 6: Enhance packages/sync-engine/src/sync-status.ts - Add SyncStatusIndicator that provides UI-friendly status data: lastSyncedAt timestamp, pendingChangesCount, isOnline boolean, conflictsCount, currentOperation string. Subscribe pattern for real-time UI updates. This builds on the existing SyncStatusManager by adding richer metadata.

Step 7: Write tests - packages/sync-engine/src/__tests__/optimistic-updates.test.ts (apply/confirm/rollback flows), packages/sync-engine/src/__tests__/offline-queue.test.ts (queue/dedup/replay/expire), packages/sync-engine/src/__tests__/retry-backoff.test.ts (retry success, exhaust retries, jitter bounds, delay calculation), packages/data-plane/src/__tests__/data-retention.test.ts (evaluate retention rules, archive batching), packages/sync-engine/src/__tests__/conflict-store.test.ts (record/resolve/history).
Step 8: Run pnpm typecheck, pnpm build, pnpm test, pnpm lint and fix any issues.
acceptance_criteria: OptimisticUpdateManager correctly applies, confirms, and rolls back mutations with subscriber notification
OfflineOperationQueue deduplicates, prioritizes, expires, and batch-replays queued operations
RetryWithBackoff correctly implements exponential backoff with jitter and respects max retries
ConflictStore records and resolves conflicts with queryable history per document
DataRetentionPolicy evaluates records against configurable retention rules
SyncStatusIndicator provides rich UI-friendly status data with subscription
All new code has tests that exercise real logic
findings: All 4 quality gates pass: typecheck 72/72, build 51/51, test 75/75, lint 61/61. Retry-backoff tests use real timers with 1ms delays to avoid fake timer issues with async promise chains. ConflictStore put in separate file rather than extending conflict-resolution.ts to keep modules focused.
